frameworks.name: Bayesian decoding
frameworks.one_line: Reconstruct posterior over stimulus from population activity using Bayes' rule.

frameworks.name: correlational approach
frameworks.one_line: Identify tuning features (width, max rate) that covary with uncertainty.

frameworks.name: normative model of ITD-based sound localization
frameworks.one_line: New normative ITD model used as testbed reproducing behavior and neural data.

frameworks.name: Bayesian inference (behavior)
frameworks.one_line: Skilled behavior shows signatures consistent with Bayesian inference.

core_question: How do neuronal populations represent the uncertainty of sensory inputs, and which analytic approach reliably identifies that representation?
key_assumptions: Brain computations underlying Bayesian behavior require accurate neural information about input uncertainty.
A normative ITD model can serve as ground truth for testing uncertainty readout methods.
Trial-by-trial uncertainty is the relevant target, not just average correlations.
manipulations: Compared two analytic frameworks (Bayesian decoding vs. correlational) on the same model.
Examined multiple candidate neural features (tuning-curve width, maximum firing rate).
key_findings: Several neural features correlated with uncertainty on average.
No single feature provided an accurate trial-by-trial uncertainty estimate.
Full population activity pattern was required to reconstruct the trial-to-trial posterior via Bayes' rule.
interpretations: Correlational approach may not reliably identify which neural aspects encode uncertainty.
Bayesian decoding of whole-population activity is the more faithful method for recovering uncertainty representations.
connecting_signals: tuning-curve width and maximum firing rate
